sglang.title: [Feat] Support Torch Symm Mem AllReduce
sglang.author: yuan-luo
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR is to support PyTorch Symm Mem AllReduce which support large message package size.
Delivers performance gains for scenarios with msg_size > 512KB.
Currently, MSCCLPP only supports a maximum message size of 1MB; messages larger than 1MB will error out.

sglang.title: [quantization] Fix scale remapping for mllama4
sglang.author: BowenBao
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Small bugfix. Previously the method always returns False regardless of whether remap happens.
This PR fix kv scale loading for ckpts that store kv_scale in naming pattern that are not .attn.k_scale / .attn.v_scale. Examples include remapping from .kv_scale or k_proj.output_scale / v_proj.output_scal

sglang.title: [quantization] Enable aiter mxfp4 fused_moe for Quark
sglang.author: BowenBao
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
aiter's moe_sorting requires topk_weights to be FP32, insert cast to fp32 before calling fused_moe.
Together with #10042 enables quark llama4 fp4 model.
Tested on ROCm MI355.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Hardware][RISC-V] Limit supported dtypes to float32 to avoid scheduler segfault
vllm.author: ihb2032
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix Issue #25655: RISC-V CPU backend crashes with segmentation fault when using float16 dtype due to a scheduler bug that provides invalid physical_block_idx values.
This PR implements a temporary workaround by restricting RISC-V platforms to only support torch.float32 dtype in vllm/platform

vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Closes #17657
This is a massive change and would be impossible to merge during the week. Any PR's merged while this was waiting for CI would cause merge conflicts which would be time consuming to solve in this PR.
Therefore, the plan to getting this PR merged quickly is as follows:

First commit:

M

vllm.title: Remove all references to `yapf` as it's no longer used
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Now that we don't have yapf fighting isort, we can remove any annotations that disabled yapf.
NOTE: There were a couple of files which had yapf globally disabled, I think this caused ruff to ignore them too. That's why the lines changed count is so high.

vllm.title: Remove all cases of `fmt: on/off`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt: It's not really necessary and in general we don't want to deviate from ruff's rules.

vllm.author: ihb2032
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR resolves a latent bug in the Mamba SSD tests identified by the Ruff linter (B023).
